name: Chengyu: Idioms & Their Stories
whatsNew: Five classic four-character idioms, each told as the short ancient story it comes from — read the tale, then use the idiom like a native. Includes tap-to-translate words and quizzes.

sentences.chinese: 从前，几个人比赛画蛇。
sentences.pinyin: Cóngqián, jǐ ge rén bǐsài huà shé.
sentences.english: Long ago, a few people held a contest to draw a snake.

sentences.chinese: 谁先画好，谁就能喝那壶酒。
sentences.pinyin: Shéi xiān huà hǎo, shéi jiù néng hē nà hú jiǔ.
sentences.english: Whoever finished first could drink the pot of wine.

sentences.chinese: 一个人很快就画好了。
sentences.pinyin: Yí ge rén hěn kuài jiù huà hǎo le.
sentences.english: One man finished very quickly.

sentences.chinese: 他觉得没意思，就给蛇添上了脚。
sentences.pinyin: Tā juéde méi yìsi, jiù gěi shé tiān shàng le jiǎo.
sentences.english: Feeling bored, he added legs to his snake.

sentences.chinese: 别人说：“蛇没有脚，你输了！”
sentences.pinyin: Biéren shuō: “Shé méiyǒu jiǎo, nǐ shū le!”
sentences.english: The others said: "Snakes have no legs — you lose!"

sentences.chinese: “画蛇添足”就是说：多做反而坏事。
sentences.pinyin: “Huà shé tiān zú” jiùshì shuō: duō zuò fǎn'ér huài shì.
sentences.english: "Drawing legs on a snake" means: doing too much ruins things.

questions.question: What was the prize in the contest?
What does 画蛇添足 mean?
options: To ruin something by overdoing it

sentences.chinese: 从前有一个农民在田里种地。
sentences.pinyin: Cóngqián yǒu yí ge nóngmín zài tián lǐ zhòng dì.
sentences.english: Long ago a farmer was working in his field.

sentences.chinese: 一只兔子撞在树桩上，死了。
sentences.pinyin: Yì zhī tùzi zhuàng zài shùzhuāng shàng, sǐ le.
sentences.english: A rabbit ran into a tree stump and died.

sentences.chinese: 农民很高兴，白白得到一只兔子。
sentences.pinyin: Nóngmín hěn gāoxìng, báibái dédào yì zhī tùzi.
sentences.english: The farmer was delighted to get a rabbit for free.

sentences.chinese: 从此他不种地了，天天守着树桩等兔子。
sentences.pinyin: Cóngcǐ tā bù zhòng dì le, tiāntiān shǒu zhe shùzhuāng děng tùzi.
sentences.english: After that he stopped farming and waited by the stump every day for rabbits.

sentences.chinese: 可是再也没有兔子来，他的田也荒了。
sentences.pinyin: Kěshì zài yě méiyǒu tùzi lái, tā de tián yě huāng le.
sentences.english: But no more rabbits came, and his field went to waste.

sentences.chinese: “守株待兔”就是说：靠运气，不肯努力。
sentences.pinyin: “Shǒu zhū dài tù” jiùshì shuō: kào yùnqì, bù kěn nǔlì.
sentences.english: "Waiting by the stump" means: relying on luck instead of effort.

questions.question: How did the farmer first get a rabbit?
What lesson does 守株待兔 teach?
options: Don't rely on luck instead of effort

sentences.chinese: 一只青蛙住在一口井里。
sentences.pinyin: Yì zhī qīngwā zhù zài yì kǒu jǐng lǐ.
sentences.english: A frog lived at the bottom of a well.

sentences.chinese: 它抬头看天，天只有井口那么大。
sentences.pinyin: Tā táitóu kàn tiān, tiān zhǐ yǒu jǐngkǒu nàme dà.
sentences.english: Looking up, it saw a sky only as big as the mouth of the well.

sentences.chinese: 它觉得自己的世界最大、最好。
sentences.pinyin: Tā juéde zìjǐ de shìjiè zuì dà, zuì hǎo.
sentences.english: It thought its own world was the biggest and best.

sentences.chinese: 一只海龟告诉它：外面的大海无边无际。
sentences.pinyin: Yì zhī hǎiguī gàosu tā: wàimiàn de dàhǎi wúbiān-wújì.
sentences.english: A sea turtle told it: the ocean outside is boundless.

sentences.chinese: 青蛙这才知道自己见得太少。
sentences.pinyin: Qīngwā zhè cái zhīdào zìjǐ jiàn de tài shǎo.
sentences.english: Only then did the frog realise how little it had seen.

sentences.chinese: “井底之蛙”就是说：眼界太小的人。
sentences.pinyin: “Jǐng dǐ zhī wā” jiùshì shuō: yǎnjiè tài xiǎo de rén.
sentences.english: "A frog in a well" means: someone with a narrow outlook.

questions.question: Why did the frog think the sky was small?
options: It only saw it through the well

questions.question: What does 井底之蛙 describe?

sentences.chinese: 一个牧民养了很多羊。
sentences.pinyin: Yí ge mùmín yǎng le hěn duō yáng.
sentences.english: A herdsman kept many sheep.

sentences.chinese: 羊圈破了一个洞，跑了一只羊。
sentences.pinyin: Yángjuàn pò le yí ge dòng, pǎo le yì zhī yáng.
sentences.english: The pen had a hole, and one sheep escaped.

sentences.chinese: 邻居劝他快修，他却不听。
sentences.pinyin: Línjū quàn tā kuài xiū, tā què bù tīng.
sentences.english: A neighbour urged him to fix it quickly, but he wouldn't listen.

sentences.chinese: 第二天，又少了一只羊。
sentences.pinyin: Dì-èr tiān, yòu shǎo le yì zhī yáng.
sentences.english: The next day, another sheep was gone.

sentences.chinese: 他赶紧把羊圈修好，再也没丢羊。
sentences.pinyin: Tā gǎnjǐn bǎ yángjuàn xiū hǎo, zài yě méi diū yáng.
sentences.english: He hurried to mend the pen, and never lost a sheep again.

sentences.chinese: “亡羊补牢”就是说：出了问题，及时改还不晚。
sentences.pinyin: “Wáng yáng bǔ láo” jiùshì shuō: chū le wèntí, jíshí gǎi hái bù wǎn.
sentences.english: "Mending the pen" means: it's not too late to fix a problem in time.

questions.question: Why did the sheep escape?
options: There was a hole in the pen

questions.question: What does 亡羊补牢 mean?
options: It's not too late to fix a problem

sentences.chinese: 古时候有一个音乐家，琴弹得很好。
sentences.pinyin: Gǔ shíhou yǒu yí ge yīnyuèjiā, qín tán de hěn hǎo.
sentences.english: In ancient times there was a musician who played beautifully.

sentences.chinese: 有一天，他对着一头牛弹琴。
sentences.pinyin: Yǒu yì tiān, tā duì zhe yì tóu niú tán qín.
sentences.english: One day he played his music facing a cow.

sentences.chinese: 他弹了最美的曲子。
sentences.pinyin: Tā tán le zuì měi de qǔzi.
sentences.english: He played his most beautiful piece.

sentences.chinese: 可是牛只顾吃草，一点也不理他。
sentences.pinyin: Kěshì niú zhǐ gù chī cǎo, yìdiǎn yě bù lǐ tā.
sentences.english: But the cow just kept eating grass, ignoring him completely.

sentences.chinese: 音乐再好，牛也听不懂。
sentences.pinyin: Yīnyuè zài hǎo, niú yě tīng bu dǒng.
sentences.english: However good the music, the cow could not understand it.

sentences.chinese: “对牛弹琴”就是说：对不懂的人白费力气。
sentences.pinyin: “Duì niú tán qín” jiùshì shuō: duì bù dǒng de rén báifèi lìqi.
sentences.english: "Playing music to a cow" means: wasting effort on the wrong audience.

questions.question: How did the cow react to the music?
options: It ignored him and ate grass

questions.question: What does 对牛弹琴 mean?
options: Wasting effort on someone who can't appreciate it

vocabulary.meaning: to ruin something by overdoing it
to trust to luck instead of effort
better late than never; fix it in time
to waste words on the wrong audience
